title: [Feature Request] About custom drawer for class which already had builtin drawer.
author: sol3breaker
body:
I had some third pary tool, which provides some class like ObscuredString, and this class had its own Drawer. So the editor Toolbox will detect there is a builtin drawer for this class. But I write an dropdown attribute for this class like 

```
[MyPop("Equip")]
public ObscuredString equipName;
```

which helps me to search in database, and I tried to integrate the custom drawer for editor toolbox, then added it to setting database. But eventually nothing happened, the function is never called, and I found the system will not handle when the class had builtin drawer. 
If the system could know there is other toolbox attribute is overwritting the builting drawer, it will fix the problem.

title: Unity 6.3, low-level physics
author: lego62451
body:
With Unity 6.3, low-level physics has been introduced. Along with it, a PhysicsMask struct was added, but it doesn’t display properly in the Inspector—it shows ‘No GUI Implemented.

Thank you for creating such a great asset.

title: Unity 6.3 warnings
author: QuiNz0r
body: Throws lots of warnings with 6.3 LTS

title: [Suggestion] ReferencePicker hierarchy list
author: iuridosanjos
body:
ReferencePicker is always showing only a flat list of elements. When it has too much elements, it gets really difficult to find anything in the list aside from searching.
IMO it would be better if it shows a hierarchy, with parents and chidren, considering namespaces.
It could be in the configurations if wanted.

It works that way in MackySoft SubclassSelector, but I improved it in my project so the common namespace gets removed and the option to remove some sufix as all of the classes I'm listing ends with the same sufix.
It uses an AdvancedDropDown, and the results be like:

some.common.namespace.different1 class1
some.common.namespace.different2 class2
some.common.namespace.different1.nested1 class3

menu shows multiple lists:
Note 1: The ">" symbol represents another list showing only the childs.
Note 2: I'm representing the indentation as a collection of classes in a single list.

|"none"
| different1 >
|— nested1 >
|—— class3
|— class1
| different2 >
|— class2

So to select class1, you should click on "different1" (namespace) > see another list of different1 childs > choose class1.

I think it's really more convenient and organized, specially when there's a good amount of classes to show. Using the namespace organization is a good way of arranging them, probably more readable.

Well, maybe it's better only to me, but I guess it's way more organized.

title: [Feature Request] - Define steps in interactable progress bars
author: zRevenger
body:
Hello,
I would like to request if possible to optionally add steps in interactable progress bars, to ensure easier and more rounded value definition even for floats.

Example of implementation:
[ProgressBar("Example", minValue: .5f, maxValue: 3f, IsInteractable = true, step: .25f)]

Currently, when using floats, you would get values that go down to multiple decimal places. For example reaching an exact 1.5 would be hard, but i'd most likely get something along the lines 1.505693

title: [Bug] ReorderableList does not respect indentation
author: p-monteiro
body: Reorderable Lists fill their parent container instead of respecting the parent indentation level. In the image example, both lists should be placed at the same level as "World Metadata" as they are declared inside of the "World" class

title: [Minor Bug] Scene Name field of Serialized Scene Misaligned in Inspector
author: snarlynarwhal
body:
Hi, I'm noticing the Scene Name field of Serialized Scene is misaligned when part of a list or serialized reference:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/89c33275-b3da-4c92-b07a-8cb501814ce7)

title: [Feature Request] Ideas for improving ReferencePicker GUI
author: snarlynarwhal
body:
Hi, I wanted to share some ideas to improve the ReferencePicker GUI:

1) It could make the inspector more compact if the dropdown went beside the label, instead of below it:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61485cdc-5312-4062-a384-2b785b612413)

2) It could also make the text less verbose to show just the type name without the namespace, preferrably nicified:

`ActionOpenUrl` or `Action Open Url` (preferred)

3) For the dropdown itself, including the namespace makes it hard to parse the options:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e04e4984-1242-45f1-ac19-85908b9f2230)

I think it can do foldouts: `HHG.Common.Runtime > Action Open Url`

Or it can list the class name first and the namespace in parenthesis: `Action Open Url (HHG.Common.Runtime)`

The [Unity-SerializeReferenceExtensions](https://github.com/mackysoft/Unity-SerializeReferenceExtensions) project has a nice gui for their `SubclassSelector` attribute which could give you further ideas:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96fa7372-ce15-490c-ab21-a0da9a702efb)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6bb82124-927c-46df-8db2-cd5a26648bb8)

I currently use their `SubclassSelector` attribute instead of `ReferencePicker` but it would be nice to not have to use an additional package just for that.

title: [Bug] EditorSettings.asset should be re-created in the Assets folder
author: snarlynarwhal
body:
It looks like the project settings edit the original EditorSettings. It should probably auto-create and edit a copy that exists in the Assets folder. This way, changed can be commited to source control. Furthermore, changes won't get lost when reinstalling the package.

- Unity 2022.3.41
- Editor Toolbox 0.14.0

```
The following asset(s) located in immutable packages were unexpectedly altered. Making changes to immutable packages isn't recommended because the changes can be lost without any warning during Package Manager operations.
  Packages/com.browar.editor-toolbox/EditorSettings.asset
```
